text: The chime of a coffee spoon against the rim of the cup. A small, accidental bell that rings once each morning, unrepeatable, depending on the angle of the wrist and how much milk remains. Nobody composes it; nobody listens on purpose. Yet it marks a threshold the way old churches once did, a clear note saying the day has properly begun. I love that the most ordinary objects keep a little music in them, waiting only to be struck, and asking nothing in return.
